title: High-cardinality label in alert rule — Prometheus cardinality explosion
code_snippet:
- alert: SlowUserRequests
  expr: histogram_quantile(0.99, sum(rate(http_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])) by (le, user_id)) > 2
  for: 5m
expected_feedback: Standard: Alert rule groups by 'user_id' — a high-cardinality label that creates a separate time series for each unique user. With 100K users, this generates 100K time series just for this one metric, consuming excessive Prometheus memory and query time. Eventually Prometheus OOMs or queries timeout. Fix: remove user_id from the grouping. Alert on aggregate service-level metrics (by endpoint, method, status_code). Use tracing (not metrics) for per-user debugging.
violated_rule: No high-cardinality labels in alert rules or dashboard queries
